name: onboarding-core-shared-api
description: src/lib/onboarding (import from @/lib/onboarding barrel) is the Phase-3 wave-1 foundation the buyer onboarding flow and admin seller-review builders both depend on. Exposes: status.ts seller_onboarding state machine (canTransition/assertTransition/allowedTransitions/transitionAction/isTerminal + OnboardingTransitionError, states from the seller_onboarding_state pgEnum, named edges submit in_progress->submitted, approve submitted->approved, reject submitted->rejected, resubmit rejected->in_progress; approved is terminal); schema.ts onboardingStepSchema (step-save) + onboardingSubmitSchema (submit) + onboardingAddressSchema; types.ts OnboardingDTO + serialize(). Dependency-light, DB/session-free.

name: step-save-vs-submit-schema-split
description: Resumable multi-step wizards get two explicit Zod schemas sharing one set of field validators: a step-save schema (every field optional, NO .default()s, so an absent field leaves the stored value untouched) and a submit schema (complete, with gate fields like termsAccepted forced true via .refine). Both built explicitly, never derived via .partial() (Zod v4 keeps .default() active on absent keys — same failure the listings edit schema hit). Structured sub-objects (address -> address_json jsonb) get a complete .strict() schema for submit and a parallel all-optional .strict() partial for step-save. Enums reused from the Drizzle pgEnum to prevent drift. Mirrors lib/listings/schema and lib/leads/schema.

name: approve-grants-additive-role-in-txn
description: Admin approval of a seller_onboarding application both transitions state (submitted->approved via @/lib/onboarding assertTransition/planApproval) AND grants the additive 'seller' role by inserting into user_roles WITHIN THE SAME db.transaction — onConflictDoNothing on the composite PK makes it idempotent and the buyer role is untouched (roles are additive, never a column; spec 3.1). Role grant + state change commit or roll back together; the applicant email is enqueued best-effort AFTER commit. Mirrors the Phase-1 listings moderation service shape (pure plan.ts + queries.ts read-side + review.ts write-side + own http.ts envelope).

content: seller_onboarding has NO reviewed_at column (schema owned by onboarding-core). The 'reviewed timestamp' for an admin approve/reject is captured by two existing facts: the updatedAt column ($onUpdate bumps it on the transition write) and the admin_audit_log row's createdAt (with a reviewSnapshot before/after). Do not add a reviewed_at column from a downstream feature — use these instead.

name: seller-profile-settings-upsert
description: Seller profile settings (spec §4.3) live at /api/dashboard/profile (GET+PUT, seller-gated via requireActorWithRole('seller')). A seller_profiles row is NOT created at role-grant time, so GET reads through getSellerProfileOrDefault(), seeding a DTO whose display_name defaults to the account name instead of 404-ing; PUT upserts on the user_id unique index. IMPORTANT: the timestamps' $onUpdate does NOT fire on insert().onConflictDoUpdate() — set updatedAt: new Date() explicitly in the set clause. Empty about/logo strings normalize to null; notification_prefs_json is a strict Zod object parsed defensively on read. display_name feeds the public 'Listed by …' line via src/lib/search/service.ts (sellerProfiles.displayName ?? users.name).

name: buyer-onboarding-route-contract
description: Buyer→seller onboarding routes (/api/me/seller-onboarding) reuse the shared core: onboardingStepSchema for step-save (upsert one seller_onboarding row, stays in_progress, merges partial address, honours leave-untouched), onboardingSubmitSchema + assertTransition(in_progress→submitted) for /submit stamping submitted_at. Step-save REOPENS a rejected app (rejected→in_progress, clearing review note) — the edit+resubmit entry point. Buyer serializer (toBuyerDTO) drops admin-internal reviewedBy via an explicit allowlist, never spread-minus-key. Already-a-seller is gated on the isSeller role (409 ALREADY_ONBOARDED), not on onboarding state; the flow never grants the role — admin approval does.

content: Client wizards may import the shared Zod schemas (@/lib/onboarding) and pgEnum runtime values (@/lib/db/schema — e.g. sellerType.enumValues) into the client bundle for consistent validation + drift-free option lists; this is the established pattern (listing-wizard does it) and builds clean. Import the buyer DTO as a type-only import so the server-only serializer module is elided from the client bundle.

name: reapprove-onboarding-is-409-terminal-not-noop
description: Re-approving an already-approved seller_onboarding row returns 409 INVALID_TRANSITION (planApproval requires the named submitted->approved edge; 'approved' is terminal), NOT a silent success. Role-grant idempotency is separate: user_roles composite PK + onConflictDoNothing means the additive seller grant never duplicates and the buyer row is retained. Verified E2E: second approve leaves exactly 1 seller role row, state still approved, and writes NO second audit row (txn rolls back). So 'idempotent re-approve' = data layer is safe, but the HTTP call is a controlled 409, not 200.
